combining_conditions_safely.label: Using Parentheses and NOT Safely
combining_conditions_safely.summary: Group mixed AND/OR logic with parentheses and use NOT to reverse a condition deliberately.

sketch1.title: NOT Reverses the Test That Follows

sc_and_requires_both.title: AND keeps rows that satisfy both checks
sc_and_requires_both.prompt: Which statement correctly describes `WHERE category = 'Art' AND price < 20`?
sc_and_requires_both.hint: Think about what `AND` requires for a row to stay in the results.

help.concept: `AND` is true only when both conditions are true for the same row.
help.hint_1: A row must match the category test and the price test at the same time.
help.hint_2: If a product is `Art` but its price is 24.50, it would not pass this filter.

options.a: It returns rows that are in Art and also cost less than 20.
options.b: It returns rows that are in Art or cost less than 20.
options.c: It returns all rows except Art products under 20.
options.d: It sorts Art products by price.

sc_parentheses_clarity.title: Parentheses make mixed logic clearer
sc_parentheses_clarity.prompt: Why is `WHERE (category = 'Art' OR category = 'Stationery') AND stock > 20` safer to read than leaving out the parentheses?
sc_parentheses_clarity.hint: Look for the answer about grouping the category conditions before checking stock.

help.concept: Parentheses group conditions so the intended logic is explicit when `AND` and `OR` appear together.
help.hint_1: The grouped part decides which category test belongs together as one unit.
help.hint_2: The safer version makes SQL check the two category options together, then apply the stock rule.

options.a: It groups the OR conditions so the stock rule applies to the whole category group.
options.b: It automatically sorts the results by stock.
options.c: It changes text values into numbers before filtering.
options.d: It removes duplicate rows from the table.

mc_queries_with_or.title: Find filters that use OR correctly
mc_queries_with_or.prompt: Which queries would return products that are either in `Art` or in `Stationery`? Choose all that apply.
mc_queries_with_or.hint: Look for conditions that accept either category value.

help.concept: `OR` keeps rows when at least one of the listed conditions is true.
help.hint_1: A correct filter must allow `Art` rows and also allow `Stationery` rows.
help.hint_2: Check whether each option compares `category` to both values with `OR`, or uses an equivalent form.

mc_parentheses_same_meaning.title: Spot equivalent grouped logic
mc_parentheses_same_meaning.prompt: Which filters mean the same as `WHERE (category = 'Art' OR category = 'Stationery') AND stock > 20`? Choose all that apply.
mc_parentheses_same_meaning.hint: The matching answers must keep the category group together and still require stock above 20.

help.concept: Equivalent logic can be written in different ways, but it must keep the same grouping and requirements.
help.hint_1: A correct option must require `stock > 20` for both allowed categories.
help.hint_2: One equivalent form repeats `stock > 20` on both sides of the `OR`; another uses `IN (...)` with the same stock check.

options.b: WHERE category = 'Art' OR category = 'Stationery' AND stock > 20
options.c: WHERE (category = 'Art' AND stock > 20) OR (category = 'Stationery' AND stock > 20)
options.d: WHERE category = 'Art' OR stock > 20 OR category = 'Stationery'

dr_build_grouped_filter.title: Build a grouped WHERE clause
dr_build_grouped_filter.prompt: Arrange the tokens to create a filter that keeps products in `Art` or `Stationery`, and only those with stock greater than 20.
dr_build_grouped_filter.hint: Focus on the logical order of the parts.

help.concept: Arrange the pieces according to the structure of the statement.
help.hint_1: Think about which part must come first.
help.hint_2: Put the pieces in the order that makes the statement valid.

dr_build_not_filter.title: Build a NOT condition safely
dr_build_not_filter.prompt: Arrange the tokens to create a filter that excludes Home products.
dr_build_not_filter.hint: Read each piece and arrange them in the order the statement should be understood.

help.concept: The pieces should form a valid statement in a logical order.
help.hint_1: Start with the piece that introduces the idea or action.
help.hint_2: Place dependent pieces after the part they describe or complete.

fb_and_keyword.title: Complete a two-part filter
fb_and_keyword.prompt: Fill in the missing keyword to keep only Stationery products that also cost less than 10.
fb_and_keyword.hint: You need the keyword that requires both conditions to be true.

help.concept: Use `the blank` when both conditions must match for the same row.
help.hint_1: The query is combining a category check with a price check.
help.hint_2: The missing word should make SQL require both the Stationery condition the blank the price condition.

fb_parenthesis_open.title: Complete the grouped condition
fb_parenthesis_open.prompt: Fill in the missing token so the category conditions are grouped before `AND stock > 20`.
fb_parenthesis_open.hint: The missing token starts the grouped part of the WHERE clause.

help.concept: Parentheses group conditions so SQL treats them as one logical unit.
help.hint_1: The category checks should be enclosed before the stock condition is applied.
help.hint_2: You need the symbol that opens a parenthesized group.

fb_parenthesis_open.template: WHERE [blank1] category = 'Art' OR category = 'Stationery') AND stock > 20

ci_art_or_stationery_stock.title: Filter grouped categories with stock
ci_art_or_stationery_stock.prompt: Write a query that shows `name`, `category`, and `stock` for products that are in `Art` or `Stationery` and have `stock > 20`. Write the query in the SQL editor, click Run query, and check the results table.
ci_art_or_stationery_stock.hint: Use the query pattern from the lesson.

help.concept: This SQL exercise checks whether your query returns the requested result.
help.hint_1: Check the table name and selected columns in the SQL editor.
help.hint_2: Run the query and compare the results table.

ci_art_or_stationery_stock.starterCode:
-- Show name, category, and stock for products that are in Art or Stationery and have stock > 20.
SELECT name, category, stock
FROM products
WHERE ;

try-combining_conditions_safely-sketch0.title: Group Alternative Categories Before Filtering Price
try-combining_conditions_safely-sketch0.prompt: Build a budget collection from the `Home` and `Art` categories. Show `name`, `category`, and `price` from `products` when the category is either one and the price is at most `30`.
try-combining_conditions_safely-sketch0.hint: Put the two category alternatives in parentheses, then join the price rule with AND.

help.concept: Parentheses make the intended grouping explicit when AND and OR appear together.
help.hint_1: Put the two category alternatives in parentheses, then join the price rule with AND.
help.hint_2: Run the query and compare the returned columns, rows, and order with the expected result.

try-combining_conditions_safely-sketch0.starterCode:
SELECT name, category, price
FROM products
-- Group the category alternatives first

try-combining_conditions_safely-sketch1.title: Reverse a Category Test with NOT
try-combining_conditions_safely-sketch1.prompt: Create a low-cost campaign list that excludes `Home`. Show `name`, `category`, and `price` from `products` where the category is not `Home` and the price is below `20`.
try-combining_conditions_safely-sketch1.hint: Wrap the category comparison in parentheses after NOT, then add the price rule with AND.

help.concept: NOT reverses the truth of the condition that follows it.
help.hint_1: Wrap the category comparison in parentheses after NOT, then add the price rule with AND.
help.hint_2: Run the query and compare the returned columns, rows, and order with the expected result.

try-combining_conditions_safely-sketch1.starterCode:
SELECT name, category, price
FROM products
-- Exclude Home and keep prices below 20

try-combining_conditions_safely-sketch2.prompt: Create a focused inventory query that shows `name`, `category`, and `stock` for products that are in `Stationery` or have `stock < 5`. Run it and compare the result with the expected table.
try-combining_conditions_safely-sketch2.hint: Use the query pattern from the lesson, then adjust the selected columns, table, or filter for this scenario.

try-combining_conditions_safely-sketch2.starterCode:
-- Show name, category, and stock for products that are in Stationery or have stock < 5.
SELECT name, category, stock
FROM products
WHERE ;

parentheses_mixed_logic.title: Parentheses Make Mixed Logic Explicit
parentheses_mixed_logic.bodyMarkdown:
When a filter contains both `AND` and `OR`, parentheses show which choices belong together.

```sql
SELECT
    name,
    category,
    price
FROM products
WHERE (category = 'Home' OR category = 'Art')
  AND price < 35;
```

SQL first accepts either category inside the parentheses. It then applies the price rule to those rows. Without the parentheses, the price limit might apply to only one side of the `OR`.

Write the grouped business idea first: “Home or Art products, and all of them must be under 35.”

not_reverses_condition.title: NOT Reverses the Test That Follows
not_reverses_condition.bodyMarkdown:
`NOT` reverses the truth of a condition.

```sql
SELECT
    name,
    category
FROM products
WHERE NOT (category = 'Office');
```

Rows in the Office category fail; other known categories pass. Parentheses make the scope of `NOT` visible and become especially useful when the reversed test contains multiple conditions.

Prefer the clearest equivalent expression. For one exact exclusion, `category <> 'Office'` is simpler; `NOT` becomes more valuable for grouped logic.
